rules.threat: A Cloud SQL instance with a public IP and open authorized networks is directly reachable from the internet for brute force and CVE exploitation.
rules.title: Cloud SQL instance must not expose a public IP
requiredActions: Set settings { ip_configuration { ipv4_enabled = false } } and connect via Private Service Connect / private IP only.

rules.threat: Without uniform bucket-level access, object ACLs can re-expose data even when IAM looks locked down.
rules.title: GCS bucket must enforce uniform bucket-level access
requiredActions: Set uniform_bucket_level_access = true on the google_storage_bucket so legacy object ACLs cannot grant access.

rules.threat: A Cloud SQL instance that does not require SSL accepts plaintext connections, exposing credentials and data on the wire.
rules.title: Cloud SQL instance must require SSL
requiredActions: Set settings { ip_configuration { require_ssl = true } } (or ssl_mode = ENCRYPTED_ONLY on newer providers) on the google_sql_database_instance.

rules.threat: GKE nodes without Shielded Nodes lack verified boot and kernel integrity, enabling rootkit persistence on a compromised node.
rules.title: GKE cluster must enable Shielded Nodes
requiredActions: Set enable_shielded_nodes = true on the google_container_cluster.

rules.threat: Without a network policy, any compromised pod can move laterally to every other pod in the cluster.
rules.title: GKE cluster must enable network policy
requiredActions: Add network_policy { enabled = true } to the google_container_cluster and enforce NetworkPolicies per namespace.

rules.threat: A Compute Engine VM without Secure Boot can be persisted by a boot-level rootkit after compromise.
rules.title: Compute instance must enable Shielded VM secure boot
requiredActions: Add shielded_instance_config { enable_secure_boot = true } to the google_compute_instance.

rules.threat: A BigQuery dataset granting allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers lets anonymous or any Google account query and exfiltrate the data.
rules.title: BigQuery dataset must not be publicly accessible

remediate.snippet: Remove the access block granting special_group = "allAuthenticatedUsers" or "allUsers". Grant explicit user/group/serviceAccount principals instead.

requiredActions: Remove the allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers access grant from the google_bigquery_dataset. Auto-fix cannot infer the correct principals — assign least-privilege access explicitly.

rules.threat: IP forwarding lets a compromised VM act as a router and intercept or redirect other instances' traffic.
rules.title: Compute instance must not enable IP forwarding
requiredActions: Set can_ip_forward = false on the google_compute_instance unless it is an intentional NAT/router appliance.

rules.threat: A Cloud SQL instance without automated backups cannot be recovered after a destructive or ransomware event.
rules.title: Cloud SQL instance must enable automated backups
requiredActions: Add settings { backup_configuration { enabled = true } } to the google_sql_database_instance.

rules.threat: A KMS key without a rotation period means a single key compromise exposes all historical ciphertext.
rules.title: KMS crypto key must set a rotation period
requiredActions: Set rotation_period = "7776000s" (90 days) or shorter on the google_kms_crypto_key.

rules.threat: A managed DNS zone without DNSSEC can be spoofed via cache poisoning, redirecting users to attacker infrastructure.
rules.title: Cloud DNS managed zone must enable DNSSEC
requiredActions: Add dnssec_config { state = "on" } to the google_dns_managed_zone.

rules.threat: A Memorystore Redis instance without AUTH accepts unauthenticated commands from anyone who can reach it on the network.
rules.title: Memorystore Redis must enable AUTH
requiredActions: Set auth_enabled = true on the google_redis_instance (and enable transit_encryption_mode).

rules.threat: GKE nodes with public IPs enlarge the attack surface and expose the kubelet/node to internet scanning.
rules.title: GKE cluster should use private nodes

remediate.snippet: Add private_cluster_config { enable_private_nodes = true, master_ipv4_cidr_block = "172.16.0.0/28" } — the master CIDR must be chosen to avoid overlap, so review before applying.

requiredActions: Enable private_cluster_config.enable_private_nodes and set a non-overlapping master_ipv4_cidr_block on the google_container_cluster.

rules.threat: A Cloud SQL authorized network of 0.0.0.0/0 exposes the database to the entire internet.
rules.title: Cloud SQL must not authorize 0.0.0.0/0

remediate.snippet: Replace the authorized_networks value 0.0.0.0/0 with specific corporate/VPC CIDRs, or disable public IP and use private IP. Auto-fix cannot choose a safe CIDR.

requiredActions: Remove the 0.0.0.0/0 authorized network from the google_sql_database_instance and use private IP or a specific allowlist.

rules.threat: Node pools without auto-repair can run degraded/compromised nodes indefinitely without remediation.
rules.title: GKE node pool must enable auto-repair
requiredActions: Add management { auto_repair = true } to the google_container_node_pool.

rules.threat: Node pools without auto-upgrade keep running known-vulnerable Kubernetes/OS versions.
rules.title: GKE node pool must enable auto-upgrade
requiredActions: Add management { auto_upgrade = true } to the google_container_node_pool.

rules.threat: A firewall rule allowing 0.0.0.0/0 ingress exposes admin/database ports to internet-wide scanning and brute force.
rules.title: Firewall must not allow 0.0.0.0/0 to admin ports

remediate.snippet: Replace source_ranges 0.0.0.0/0 with specific corporate/VPC CIDRs, or use IAP for TCP forwarding for 22/3389. Auto-fix cannot choose a safe source range.

requiredActions: Restrict source_ranges on the google_compute_firewall (especially for SSH/RDP/DB ports).

rules.threat: Without Binary Authorization, unsigned or untrusted container images can be deployed to the cluster.
rules.title: GKE cluster should enable Binary Authorization

remediate.snippet: Add binary_authorization { evaluation_mode = "PROJECT_SINGLETON_POLICY_ENFORCE" } and define attestors. Policy design requires review.

requiredActions: Enable binary_authorization with policy enforcement on the google_container_cluster.

rules.threat: Without OS Login, SSH keys are managed in metadata rather than IAM, weakening access control and auditing.
rules.title: Compute instance should enable OS Login

remediate.snippet: Set metadata = { enable-oslogin = "TRUE" } on the instance (or enforce the org policy). Auto-fix avoids editing the metadata map to prevent clobbering other keys.

requiredActions: Enable OS Login via metadata enable-oslogin = TRUE (prefer the org-level constraint).

rules.threat: A KMS key IAM binding to allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers lets anyone use the key to decrypt or sign.
rules.title: KMS key must not be publicly accessible

remediate.snippet: Remove the allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers member from the KMS key IAM binding and grant explicit principals.

requiredActions: Remove public members from the google_kms_crypto_key_iam_member binding.

rules.threat: Without intranode visibility, pod-to-pod traffic on the same node bypasses VPC flow logging and firewall enforcement.
rules.title: GKE cluster should enable intranode visibility
requiredActions: Set enable_intranode_visibility = true on the google_container_cluster.

rules.threat: GKE nodes without Secure Boot can be persisted by a boot-level rootkit after compromise.
rules.title: GKE node pool should enable Secure Boot
requiredActions: Add shielded_instance_config { enable_secure_boot = true } to the google_container_node_pool.

rules.threat: A user-managed service account key is a long-lived static credential with no expiry — the top GCP breach vector.
rules.title: Avoid user-managed service account keys

remediate.snippet: Remove the google_service_account_key and use Workload Identity / Workload Identity Federation (keyless) instead.

requiredActions: Eliminate the google_service_account_key; adopt Workload Identity Federation.

rules.threat: Granting roles/owner or roles/editor at the project level gives broad, hard-to-audit privilege — ideal for lateral movement.
rules.title: Avoid primitive roles/owner and roles/editor bindings

remediate.snippet: Replace roles/owner or roles/editor with a least-privilege predefined or custom role. Auto-fix cannot infer the minimal role required.

requiredActions: Replace the primitive role in google_project_iam_member with a least-privilege role.

rules.threat: A Cloud Run service granting allUsers the invoker role is an unauthenticated internet endpoint.
rules.title: Cloud Run service must not allow unauthenticated (allUsers) invocation

remediate.snippet: Remove the allUsers invoker binding and front the service with IAP / an authenticated gateway, or grant specific principals.

requiredActions: Remove the allUsers run.invoker binding from the google_cloud_run_service_iam_member.

rules.threat: Without point-in-time recovery, a Cloud SQL instance cannot be restored to a moment before a destructive or ransomware event.
rules.title: Cloud SQL instance should enable point-in-time recovery
requiredActions: Set settings { backup_configuration { point_in_time_recovery_enabled = true } } on the google_sql_database_instance (Postgres) or enable binary logging (MySQL).

rules.threat: A cluster not subscribed to a release channel falls behind on security patches and CVE fixes.
rules.title: GKE cluster should subscribe to a release channel
requiredActions: Add release_channel { channel = "REGULAR" } to the google_container_cluster.

rules.threat: A Dataproc cluster with public IPs on its nodes enlarges the attack surface and exposes workers to the internet.
rules.title: Dataproc cluster should use internal IPs only

remediate.snippet: Set cluster_config { gce_cluster_config { internal_ip_only = true } } — this requires Private Google Access on the subnet, so apply with networking review.

requiredActions: Enable internal_ip_only on the google_dataproc_cluster (requires Private Google Access).

rules.threat: A bucket without a customer-managed encryption key relies on Google-managed keys, reducing key control and revocation.
rules.title: GCS bucket should use a customer-managed encryption key

remediate.snippet: Add encryption { default_kms_key_name = <kms-key> } to the google_storage_bucket. The CMEK resource must be supplied.

requiredActions: Configure CMEK via encryption.default_kms_key_name on the google_storage_bucket.

rules.threat: A Compute instance with an external IP (access_config) is directly reachable from the internet, widening the attack surface.
rules.title: Compute instance should not have a public IP

remediate.snippet: Remove the access_config block from the network_interface to drop the external IP; reach the VM via IAP or a bastion. Auto-fix avoids deleting blocks.

requiredActions: Remove the access_config (external IP) from the google_compute_instance network_interface.

rules.threat: A Vertex AI / AI Platform notebook with a public IP is internet-reachable and a prime target for compromise and data exfiltration.
rules.title: AI notebook instance must not have a public IP
requiredActions: Set no_public_ip = true on the google_notebooks_instance.

rules.threat: A GKE control plane reachable from any IP is exposed to credential stuffing and API exploitation.
rules.title: GKE cluster should restrict master authorized networks

remediate.snippet: Add master_authorized_networks_config { cidr_blocks { cidr_block = <admin-cidr> } }. The allowed CIDRs must be chosen by a human.

requiredActions: Restrict the GKE control plane via master_authorized_networks_config.

rules.threat: Without application-layer secrets encryption, Kubernetes Secrets are stored only base64-encoded in etcd.
rules.title: GKE cluster should enable application-layer secrets encryption

remediate.snippet: Add database_encryption { state = "ENCRYPTED", key_name = <kms-key> }. A Cloud KMS key must be supplied.

requiredActions: Enable database_encryption (ENCRYPTED, with a KMS key) on the google_container_cluster.

rules.threat: Without Confidential VM, memory is not encrypted in use — a hypervisor-level compromise can read sensitive in-memory data.
rules.title: Compute instance may enable Confidential VM

remediate.snippet: Add confidential_instance_config { enable_confidential_compute = true } and use an N2D/C2D machine type with on_host_maintenance = TERMINATE. Machine-type constraints require review.

requiredActions: Enable Confidential VM (confidential_instance_config) on a supported machine type.

rules.threat: A GKE client certificate is a long-lived, non-revocable credential that bypasses IAM if it leaks.
rules.title: GKE cluster must disable client certificate auth
requiredActions: Set master_auth { client_certificate_config { issue_client_certificate = false } } on the google_container_cluster.

rules.threat: A Pub/Sub topic without a CMK relies on Google-managed keys, reducing key control over message data at rest.
rules.title: Pub/Sub topic should use a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Set kms_key_name to a Cloud KMS key on the google_pubsub_topic. The CMEK resource must be supplied.

requiredActions: Configure CMEK via kms_key_name on the google_pubsub_topic.

rules.threat: A Cloud Function accepting all ingress is internet-exposed; internal-only ingress shrinks the attack surface.
rules.title: Cloud Function should restrict ingress

remediate.snippet: Set service_config { ingress_settings = "ALLOW_INTERNAL_ONLY" } (or ALLOW_INTERNAL_AND_GCLB) unless the function must be public.

requiredActions: Restrict ingress_settings on the google_cloudfunctions2_function.

rules.threat: A Dataproc cluster without CMEK relies on Google-managed keys for PD and job data at rest.
rules.title: Dataproc cluster should use a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Set cluster_config { encryption_config { gce_pd_kms_key_name = <kms-key> } } on the google_dataproc_cluster.

requiredActions: Configure CMEK (gce_pd_kms_key_name) on the google_dataproc_cluster.

rules.threat: Without VPC flow logs there is no network-level record of exfiltration or lateral movement between subnets.
rules.title: VPC subnet should enable flow logs

remediate.snippet: Add log_config { aggregation_interval = "INTERVAL_5_SEC", flow_sampling = 0.5, metadata = "INCLUDE_ALL_METADATA" } to the google_compute_subnetwork.

requiredActions: Enable log_config (flow logs) on the google_compute_subnetwork.

rules.threat: Allowing project-wide SSH keys widens blast radius: any project SSH key can log into this VM.
rules.title: Compute instance should block project-wide SSH keys

remediate.snippet: Set metadata = { block-project-ssh-keys = "true" } (or enforce OS Login). Auto-fix avoids editing the metadata map to prevent clobbering other keys.

requiredActions: Block project-wide SSH keys on the google_compute_instance (prefer OS Login).

rules.threat: A BigQuery dataset without a customer-managed key relies on Google-managed keys, reducing key control over data at rest.
rules.title: BigQuery dataset should use a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Add default_encryption_configuration { kms_key_name = <kms-key> } to the google_bigquery_dataset. The CMEK key must be supplied.

requiredActions: Configure CMEK (default_encryption_configuration) on the google_bigquery_dataset.

rules.threat: A persistent disk without a customer-managed key relies on Google-managed keys, reducing key control over data at rest.
rules.title: Compute disk should use a customer-managed encryption key

remediate.snippet: Add disk_encryption_key { kms_key_self_link = <kms-key> } to the google_compute_disk. The CMEK key must be supplied.

requiredActions: Configure CMEK (disk_encryption_key) on the google_compute_disk.

rules.threat: A Spanner database without CMEK relies on Google-managed keys for data at rest.
rules.title: Spanner database should use a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Add encryption_config { kms_key_name = <kms-key> } to the google_spanner_database.

requiredActions: Configure CMEK (encryption_config) on the google_spanner_database.

rules.threat: An unlocked log bucket can be shortened or purged by an attacker covering tracks; a locked retention prevents tampering.
rules.title: Logging bucket should lock its retention policy

remediate.snippet: Set locked = true and a retention_days on the google_logging_project_bucket_config. Locking is irreversible, so apply deliberately.

requiredActions: Lock the retention policy on the google_logging_project_bucket_config (irreversible).

rules.threat: A Secret Manager secret without CMEK relies on Google-managed keys, reducing key control and revocation over secret material.
rules.title: Secret Manager secret should use a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Add replication { user_managed { replicas { customer_managed_encryption { kms_key_name = <kms-key> } } } } to the google_secret_manager_secret.

requiredActions: Configure CMEK (customer_managed_encryption) on the google_secret_manager_secret.

rules.threat: An Artifact Registry without CMEK relies on Google-managed keys for stored images/packages at rest.
rules.title: Artifact Registry should use a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Set kms_key_name = <kms-key> on the google_artifact_registry_repository.

requiredActions: Configure CMEK (kms_key_name) on the google_artifact_registry_repository.

rules.threat: Non-COS node images have a larger attack surface and weaker default hardening than Container-Optimized OS.
rules.title: GKE node pool should use a Container-Optimized OS image

remediate.snippet: Set node_config { image_type = "COS_CONTAINERD" } on the google_container_node_pool.

requiredActions: Use a COS/COS_CONTAINERD image_type on the google_container_node_pool.

rules.threat: Legacy ABAC grants broad, static permissions that bypass fine-grained RBAC; any leaked node or pod token inherits sweeping cluster access.
rules.title: GKE cluster must not enable legacy ABAC

remediate.snippet: Remove enable_legacy_abac or set enable_legacy_abac { enabled = false } on the google_container_cluster and rely on RBAC.

requiredActions: Disable legacy ABAC on the google_container_cluster; enforce authorization via RBAC.

rules.threat: Without object versioning, a malicious or accidental overwrite/delete of bucket objects is unrecoverable — no ransomware rollback.
rules.title: Storage bucket should enable object versioning

remediate.snippet: Add versioning { enabled = true } to the google_storage_bucket.

requiredActions: Add versioning { enabled = true } to the google_storage_bucket.

rules.threat: Without access/storage logging, bulk object reads (data exfiltration) leave no audit trail on the bucket.
rules.title: Storage bucket should enable access logging

remediate.snippet: Add a logging { log_bucket = <dedicated-log-bucket> } block to the google_storage_bucket pointing at a separate, access-restricted bucket.

requiredActions: Add a logging block to the google_storage_bucket targeting a dedicated log bucket.

rules.threat: An enabled interactive serial console lets anyone with the project metadata permission attach and gain interactive access to the instance, bypassing SSH controls.
rules.title: Compute instance must disable serial port access

remediate.snippet: Remove serial-port-enable from metadata or set it to "false" on the google_compute_instance.

requiredActions: Set metadata serial-port-enable = "false" (or remove it) on the google_compute_instance.

rules.threat: A GKE cluster with a public control-plane endpoint exposes the Kubernetes API server to internet-wide credential stuffing and CVE exploitation.
rules.title: GKE cluster should enable a private control-plane endpoint

remediate.snippet: Set private_cluster_config { enable_private_endpoint = true } and configure master_authorized_networks on the google_container_cluster. Networking choices require review.

requiredActions: Enable a private control-plane endpoint (private_cluster_config.enable_private_endpoint) on the google_container_cluster.

rules.threat: Without a virtual TPM, the instance cannot attest boot integrity, so a rootkit altering the boot chain goes undetected.
rules.title: Compute instance should enable vTPM

remediate.snippet: Add shielded_instance_config { enable_vtpm = true } to the google_compute_instance.

requiredActions: Add shielded_instance_config { enable_vtpm = true } to the google_compute_instance.

rules.threat: A Cloud SQL instance without a customer-managed encryption key is encrypted only with Google-managed keys, leaving no customer-controlled revocation if the data must be cryptographically shredded.
rules.title: Cloud SQL instance should use a customer-managed encryption key

remediate.snippet: Set encryption_key_name to a Cloud KMS CryptoKey on the google_sql_database_instance (CMEK can only be set at creation).

requiredActions: Set encryption_key_name (a Cloud KMS key) on the google_sql_database_instance.

rules.threat: DNSSEC signing with the broken RSASHA1 algorithm lets an attacker who can forge signatures spoof DNS responses for the zone.
rules.title: DNS zone must not sign DNSSEC with RSASHA1

remediate.snippet: Use a stronger DNSSEC algorithm (e.g. rsasha256 or ecdsap256sha256) in default_key_specs on the google_dns_managed_zone.

requiredActions: Replace rsasha1 with rsasha256/ecdsap256sha256 in the DNSSEC key specs of the google_dns_managed_zone.

rules.threat: A Cloud Run service that accepts all ingress is directly reachable from the internet, widening the attack surface beyond a load balancer or internal callers.
rules.title: Cloud Run service should restrict ingress to internal/load-balancer

remediate.snippet: Set ingress = "INGRESS_TRAFFIC_INTERNAL_ONLY" (or INTERNAL_LOAD_BALANCER) on the google_cloud_run_v2_service unless it must be public.

requiredActions: Restrict ingress on the google_cloud_run_v2_service to internal or load-balancer traffic.

rules.threat: Without Workload Identity, pods authenticate using the node service account or exported keys, so a compromised pod inherits broad node-level GCP permissions.
rules.title: GKE cluster should enable Workload Identity

remediate.snippet: Add workload_identity_config { workload_pool = "<project>.svc.id.goog" } to the google_container_cluster and bind KSAs to GSAs.

requiredActions: Enable workload_identity_config on the google_container_cluster so pods use scoped identities, not the node SA.

rules.threat: Without enforced public access prevention, a future IAM binding or ACL can grant allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers and silently expose the bucket.
rules.title: Storage bucket must enforce public access prevention
requiredActions: Set public_access_prevention = "enforced" on the google_storage_bucket.

rules.threat: A Cloud Function IAM binding granting roles/cloudfunctions.invoker to allUsers makes the function an unauthenticated internet endpoint reaching its service account.
rules.title: Cloud Function must not grant invoker to allUsers

remediate.snippet: Replace member = "allUsers"/"allAuthenticatedUsers" with specific user/service-account principals on the google_cloudfunctions2_function_iam_member.

requiredActions: Remove allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers from the Cloud Function invoker binding; grant specific principals.

rules.threat: An instance running as the default Compute Engine service account with the cloud-platform scope grants any code on the VM full API access to the project. A single RCE becomes total project compromise.
rules.title: Do not grant the cloud-platform scope to Compute instances

remediate.snippet: Attach a dedicated least-privilege service account and replace the broad cloud-platform scope with narrowly scoped OAuth scopes (or rely on IAM roles).

requiredActions: Replace the cloud-platform scope on the instance service_account block with the minimal scopes the workload needs.

rules.threat: Without integrity monitoring a rootkit or bootkit altering the VM boot sequence goes undetected, letting an attacker persist beneath the OS.
rules.title: Enable Shielded VM integrity monitoring
requiredActions: Set shielded_instance_config.enable_integrity_monitoring = true on the instance.

rules.threat: Adding SSH keys directly in instance metadata bypasses OS Login's IAM-based access control and audit logging, letting stale or shared keys grant lingering shell access.
rules.title: Manage SSH access via OS Login, not metadata ssh-keys

remediate.snippet: Remove the ssh-keys metadata entry and enable OS Login (enable-oslogin = TRUE) so access is governed by IAM.

requiredActions: Remove ssh-keys from instance metadata and rely on OS Login for SSH access.

rules.threat: Without deletion protection an attacker or accidental Terraform run can destroy a production instance, causing data loss and outage.
rules.title: Enable deletion protection on Compute instances
requiredActions: Set deletion_protection = true on the instance.

rules.threat: A firewall rule allowing 3389/tcp from 0.0.0.0/0 exposes RDP to the internet, inviting brute-force and BlueKeep-style exploitation.
rules.title: Do not expose RDP (3389) to the internet

remediate.snippet: Restrict source_ranges to trusted CIDRs or use IAP TCP forwarding instead of opening 3389 to 0.0.0.0/0.

requiredActions: Restrict the RDP firewall rule source_ranges to known administrative IPs or remove port 3389.

rules.threat: Firewall rules without logging leave no record of allowed or denied connections, blinding incident responders to lateral movement and exfiltration attempts.
rules.title: Enable logging on firewall rules

remediate.snippet: Add a log_config block with metadata = "INCLUDE_ALL_METADATA" to the firewall rule.

requiredActions: Add a log_config block to the firewall rule to enable connection logging.

rules.threat: An egress allow rule to 0.0.0.0/0 lets a compromised instance exfiltrate data and reach command-and-control servers anywhere on the internet.
rules.title: Restrict unrestricted egress firewall rules

remediate.snippet: Scope egress destination_ranges to required destinations rather than 0.0.0.0/0.

requiredActions: Limit the egress firewall rule destination_ranges to required CIDRs.

rules.threat: Without Private Google Access, VMs without external IPs cannot reach Google APIs privately, pushing operators to attach public IPs and widening the attack surface.
rules.title: Enable Private Google Access on subnets
requiredActions: Set private_ip_google_access = true on the subnetwork.

rules.threat: A COMPATIBLE or custom SSL policy permits weak ciphers and old TLS, enabling downgrade and decryption attacks against traffic to load balancers.
rules.title: Use a MODERN or RESTRICTED SSL policy profile
requiredActions: Set the SSL policy profile to MODERN or RESTRICTED.

rules.threat: An SSL policy allowing TLS 1.0/1.1 leaves load-balanced services open to POODLE, BEAST, and protocol-downgrade attacks.
rules.title: Require a minimum TLS version of 1.2 on SSL policies
requiredActions: Set min_tls_version = "TLS_1_2" on the SSL policy.

rules.threat: A target HTTPS proxy without an attached SSL policy falls back to permissive defaults, allowing weak TLS negotiation with clients.
rules.title: Attach an SSL policy to target HTTPS proxies

remediate.snippet: Reference a hardened google_compute_ssl_policy via the ssl_policy argument.

requiredActions: Set ssl_policy on the target HTTPS proxy to a MODERN/RESTRICTED policy.

rules.threat: A backend service without a Cloud Armor security policy has no WAF or DDoS layer, leaving applications exposed to L7 floods and injection attacks.
rules.title: Attach a Cloud Armor security policy to backend services

remediate.snippet: Reference a google_compute_security_policy via the security_policy argument.

requiredActions: Set security_policy on the backend service to a Cloud Armor policy.

rules.threat: Backend services without logging provide no visibility into request patterns, hampering detection of scraping, abuse, and exploitation.
rules.title: Enable logging on backend services

remediate.snippet: Add a log_config block with enable = true and an appropriate sample_rate.

requiredActions: Add a log_config block with enable = true to the backend service.

rules.threat: Cloud NAT without logging hides outbound connections from compromised instances, eliminating a key exfiltration and C2 detection signal.
rules.title: Enable Cloud NAT logging

remediate.snippet: Add a log_config block with enable = true and filter = "ALL".

requiredActions: Add a log_config block to the Cloud NAT with enable = true.

rules.threat: DNS policies without query logging miss the DNS-based exfiltration and tunneling signals that often reveal compromised workloads.
rules.title: Enable DNS query logging on DNS policies
requiredActions: Set enable_logging = true on the DNS policy.

rules.threat: A GKE cluster with logging disabled loses audit and container logs, blinding responders to in-cluster attacks and lateral movement.
rules.title: Enable Cloud Logging on GKE clusters
requiredActions: Set logging_service to logging.googleapis.com/kubernetes (or configure logging_config).

rules.threat: Disabling GKE monitoring removes metric-based anomaly detection, letting cryptominers and runaway compromised pods go unnoticed.
rules.title: Enable Cloud Monitoring on GKE clusters
requiredActions: Set monitoring_service to monitoring.googleapis.com/kubernetes (or configure monitoring_config).

rules.threat: Without Confidential GKE nodes, in-memory data is unencrypted and readable by a host-level attacker or malicious insider with hypervisor access.
rules.title: Enable Confidential Nodes on GKE clusters

remediate.snippet: Add a confidential_nodes block with enabled = true (requires N2D/C2D machine types).

requiredActions: Add confidential_nodes { enabled = true } to the cluster.

rules.threat: Without security posture scanning, GKE workload misconfigurations and known-vulnerable images deploy unflagged, expanding the cluster attack surface.
rules.title: Enable GKE security posture config

remediate.snippet: Add a security_posture_config block with mode = "BASIC" and vulnerability_mode set.

requiredActions: Add a security_posture_config block to the cluster.

rules.threat: Node pools exposing the legacy metadata endpoints let a compromised pod read the node's service-account token and bootstrap credentials, escalating to cluster takeover.
rules.title: Use GKE_METADATA workload metadata mode on node pools

remediate.snippet: Set workload_metadata_config { mode = "GKE_METADATA" } so Workload Identity shields the node metadata server.

requiredActions: Set workload_metadata_config mode to GKE_METADATA on the node pool.

rules.threat: Untrusted workloads on a node pool without gVisor share the host kernel, so a container-escape exploit reaches the node and other tenants.
rules.title: Enable gVisor sandboxing for untrusted node pools

remediate.snippet: Add sandbox_config { sandbox_type = "gvisor" } for node pools running untrusted code.

requiredActions: Add sandbox_config { sandbox_type = "gvisor" } to node pools running untrusted workloads.

rules.threat: GKE nodes without integrity monitoring cannot detect boot-level tampering, allowing a node rootkit to persist and harvest secrets.
rules.title: Enable integrity monitoring on GKE node pools
requiredActions: Set shielded_instance_config.enable_integrity_monitoring = true on the node pool.

rules.threat: Node pools using the default Compute service account inherit broad project permissions, so a single pod compromise can pivot across the project.
rules.title: Assign a dedicated least-privilege service account to node pools

remediate.snippet: Set node_config.service_account to a custom least-privilege service account instead of the default.

requiredActions: Set a dedicated minimal service_account in the node pool node_config.

rules.threat: Without log_connections, successful and failed Postgres logins are unrecorded, removing the audit trail needed to spot credential-stuffing and unauthorized access.
rules.title: Enable Postgres log_connections on Cloud SQL

remediate.snippet: Add a database_flags block { name = "log_connections" value = "on" }.

requiredActions: Add the log_connections=on database flag to the Cloud SQL instance.

rules.threat: Without log_disconnections, session end times are missing, making it hard to reconstruct an attacker's session duration during incident response.
rules.title: Enable Postgres log_disconnections on Cloud SQL

remediate.snippet: Add a database_flags block { name = "log_disconnections" value = "on" }.

requiredActions: Add the log_disconnections=on database flag to the Cloud SQL instance.

rules.threat: Disabled log_checkpoints reduces operational forensic data, weakening the ability to correlate database activity during an investigation.
rules.title: Enable Postgres log_checkpoints on Cloud SQL

remediate.snippet: Add a database_flags block { name = "log_checkpoints" value = "on" }.

requiredActions: Add the log_checkpoints=on database flag to the Cloud SQL instance.

rules.threat: Without log_lock_waits, lock contention from injection-driven or abusive queries goes unlogged, masking denial-of-service patterns.
rules.title: Enable Postgres log_lock_waits on Cloud SQL

remediate.snippet: Add a database_flags block { name = "log_lock_waits" value = "on" }.

requiredActions: Add the log_lock_waits=on database flag to the Cloud SQL instance.

rules.threat: An overly permissive log_min_messages setting can suppress warning-level events that reveal probing or misuse of the database.
rules.title: Set Postgres log_min_messages appropriately on Cloud SQL

remediate.snippet: Add a database_flags block { name = "log_min_messages" value = "warning" } or stricter.

requiredActions: Add the log_min_messages database flag set to warning or stricter.

rules.threat: Contained database authentication on SQL Server lets users authenticate without a server login, enabling lateral movement between databases and bypassing central control.
rules.title: Disable contained database authentication on Cloud SQL Server

remediate.snippet: Set the 'contained database authentication' database flag to off.

requiredActions: Set the 'contained database authentication' flag to off on the SQL Server instance.

rules.threat: Cross db ownership chaining lets an attacker who controls one database escalate privileges into others sharing an owner, breaking database isolation.
rules.title: Disable cross db ownership chaining on Cloud SQL Server

remediate.snippet: Set the 'cross db ownership chaining' database flag to off.

requiredActions: Set the 'cross db ownership chaining' flag to off on the SQL Server instance.

rules.threat: MySQL local_infile lets clients read server-side files, which an attacker exploiting SQL injection can abuse to exfiltrate arbitrary files from the host.
rules.title: Disable local_infile on Cloud SQL MySQL

remediate.snippet: Set the local_infile database flag to off.

requiredActions: Set the local_infile flag to off on the MySQL instance.

rules.threat: Without skip_show_database, any MySQL user can enumerate all database names, giving an attacker a map of targets for further compromise.
rules.title: Enable skip_show_database on Cloud SQL MySQL

remediate.snippet: Add a database_flags block { name = "skip_show_database" value = "on" }.

requiredActions: Add the skip_show_database=on database flag to the MySQL instance.

rules.threat: With deletion protection off, an attacker with API access or an errant Terraform apply can permanently destroy the production database.
rules.title: Enable deletion protection on Cloud SQL instances
requiredActions: Set deletion_protection = true on the Cloud SQL instance.

rules.threat: Buckets without a retention policy allow an attacker who gains write access to overwrite or delete objects, defeating ransomware recovery and tamper-evidence.
rules.title: Configure a retention policy on storage buckets

remediate.snippet: Add a retention_policy block with an appropriate retention_period for immutable data.

requiredActions: Add a retention_policy block to the bucket where immutability is required.

rules.threat: Software-protected KMS keys are more exposed than HSM-backed keys; an attacker with sufficient access has a larger window to extract or misuse high-value keys.
rules.title: Use HSM protection level for sensitive KMS keys

remediate.snippet: Set version_template { protection_level = "HSM" } for high-value keys.

requiredActions: Set protection_level = "HSM" in the key's version_template for sensitive keys.

rules.threat: A short destroy_scheduled_duration shrinks the recovery window, so an attacker who initiates key destruction can render data unrecoverable before defenders react.
rules.title: Set an adequate destroy scheduled duration on KMS keys

remediate.snippet: Set destroy_scheduled_duration to at least 86400s (24h) to retain a recovery window.

requiredActions: Set destroy_scheduled_duration to an adequate value (e.g. 86400s) on the crypto key.

rules.threat: Secrets without automatic rotation stay valid indefinitely, so a leaked credential remains usable to an attacker long after exposure.
rules.title: Configure rotation on Secret Manager secrets

remediate.snippet: Add a rotation block with rotation_period and a topics notification target.

requiredActions: Add a rotation block to the Secret Manager secret.

rules.threat: Secrets without an expiry persist forever, increasing the chance an old, leaked secret is still accepted by services.
rules.title: Set an expiry on ephemeral Secret Manager secrets

remediate.snippet: Set expire_time or ttl on short-lived secrets so they auto-expire.

requiredActions: Set expire_time or ttl on ephemeral Secret Manager secrets.

rules.threat: Without DATA_READ/DATA_WRITE audit logging, reads and writes to sensitive resources go unrecorded, so data exfiltration leaves no trace.
rules.title: Enable Data Access audit logging

remediate.snippet: Add audit_log_config blocks for DATA_READ and DATA_WRITE log types covering allServices.

requiredActions: Add DATA_READ and DATA_WRITE audit_log_config blocks to the audit config.

rules.threat: Exempting members from audit logging creates blind spots an insider or attacker can hide activity behind.
rules.title: Do not exempt members from audit logging

remediate.snippet: Remove exempted_members so all principals are subject to audit logging.

requiredActions: Remove exempted_members from the audit_log_config blocks.

rules.threat: Topics without message retention cannot replay events for forensic review after an incident, losing evidence of malicious or anomalous messages.
rules.title: Configure message retention on Pub/Sub topics

remediate.snippet: Set message_retention_duration on the topic to retain messages for replay/forensics.

requiredActions: Set message_retention_duration on the Pub/Sub topic.

rules.threat: Granting allUsers or allAuthenticatedUsers on a subscription lets anyone consume messages, exposing potentially sensitive event data.
rules.title: Do not grant public access to Pub/Sub subscriptions

remediate.snippet: Replace allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers with specific service accounts or groups.

requiredActions: Remove allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers bindings from the subscription IAM member.

rules.threat: A Memorystore Redis instance without in-transit encryption sends cache data in cleartext, letting a network attacker sniff or tamper with cached secrets and sessions.
rules.title: Enable in-transit encryption on Memorystore Redis
requiredActions: Set transit_encryption_mode = "SERVER_AUTHENTICATION" on the Redis instance.

rules.threat: A Redis instance in DIRECT_PEERING or with a public-facing connect mode is more exposed than one using PRIVATE_SERVICE_ACCESS, widening reachability for attackers.
rules.title: Use PRIVATE_SERVICE_ACCESS connect mode for Memorystore Redis
requiredActions: Set connect_mode = "PRIVATE_SERVICE_ACCESS" on the Redis instance.

rules.threat: BigQuery tables without customer-managed encryption rely solely on Google-managed keys, removing the customer's ability to revoke access by disabling the key during a breach.
rules.title: Use CMEK on BigQuery tables

remediate.snippet: Add an encryption_configuration block with kms_key_name set to a customer-managed key.

requiredActions: Add encryption_configuration with kms_key_name to the BigQuery table.

rules.threat: Dataflow workers with public IPs are directly reachable from the internet, exposing the job's processing nodes to network attacks.
rules.title: Disable public IPs on Dataflow jobs
requiredActions: Set ip_configuration = "WORKER_IP_PRIVATE" on the Dataflow job.

rules.threat: Dataproc clusters without Shielded VM config lack secure boot and vTPM protections, exposing cluster nodes to boot-level tampering.
rules.title: Enable Shielded VM config on Dataproc clusters

remediate.snippet: Add a shielded_instance_config block enabling secure boot, vTPM, and integrity monitoring.

requiredActions: Add a shielded_instance_config block to the Dataproc cluster gce_cluster_config.

rules.threat: A Cloud Function without a VPC connector egresses over the public internet, preventing private routing and exposing outbound traffic to interception.
rules.title: Route Cloud Functions egress through a VPC connector

remediate.snippet: Set vpc_connector to a Serverless VPC Access connector for private egress.

requiredActions: Set vpc_connector on the Cloud Function for private VPC egress.

rules.threat: Granting allUsers the invoker role on a 1st-gen Cloud Function makes it publicly invocable, exposing the function logic and any downstream access to anyone.
rules.title: Do not allow public invocation of Cloud Functions

remediate.snippet: Replace allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers with specific authenticated principals.

requiredActions: Remove allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers from the function invoker IAM member.

rules.threat: Binding allUsers or allAuthenticatedUsers to a storage bucket exposes its objects to the entire internet, the classic GCS data-leak vector.
rules.title: Do not grant public access to storage buckets

remediate.snippet: Remove allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers bindings and grant access to specific principals.

requiredActions: Remove allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers from the bucket IAM member binding.

rules.threat: Public IAM bindings on a BigQuery dataset let anyone query potentially sensitive analytics data.
rules.title: Do not grant public access to BigQuery datasets via IAM

remediate.snippet: Remove allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers and grant dataset access to specific principals.

requiredActions: Remove public members from the BigQuery dataset IAM member binding.

rules.threat: Granting primitive owner/editor roles at the project level via binding gives broad standing access that an attacker can abuse for full project control.
rules.title: Avoid primitive roles in project IAM bindings

remediate.snippet: Replace roles/owner and roles/editor with granular predefined or custom roles.

requiredActions: Replace primitive roles in the project IAM binding with least-privilege roles.

rules.threat: A Spanner database without deletion protection can be destroyed by a compromised credential or stray Terraform apply, causing irreversible data loss.
rules.title: Enable deletion protection on Spanner databases
requiredActions: Set deletion_protection = true on the Spanner database.

rules.threat: A Bigtable instance without deletion protection can be wiped by an attacker or accidental destroy, losing all stored data.
rules.title: Enable deletion protection on Bigtable instances
requiredActions: Set deletion_protection = true on the Bigtable instance.

rules.threat: A disk image granted to allAuthenticatedUsers is downloadable by any Google account, leaking baked-in secrets, code, and credentials.
rules.title: Do not share Compute images publicly

remediate.snippet: Remove allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers from the image IAM binding and share with specific principals only.

requiredActions: Remove public members from the Compute image IAM member binding.

rules.threat: Public IAM on a KMS key ring lets anyone use the contained keys to encrypt/decrypt, breaking the confidentiality of every object protected by those keys.
rules.title: Do not grant public access to KMS key rings

remediate.snippet: Remove allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers from the key ring IAM binding.

requiredActions: Remove public members from the KMS key ring IAM member binding.

rules.threat: A Filestore instance without CMEK relies only on Google-managed keys, removing the customer's ability to revoke data access by disabling a key during a breach.
rules.title: Use CMEK on Filestore instances
requiredActions: Set kms_key_name on the Filestore instance to a customer-managed key.

rules.threat: Vertex AI featurestores without CMEK depend solely on Google-managed encryption, removing the key-revocation control during incident response.
rules.title: Use CMEK on Vertex AI featurestores

remediate.snippet: Add an encryption_spec block with kms_key_name referencing a customer-managed key.

requiredActions: Add encryption_spec with kms_key_name to the Vertex AI featurestore.

rules.threat: Instance templates without Shielded VM config propagate insecure boot settings to every VM in a managed instance group, scaling the boot-tampering risk fleet-wide.
rules.title: Enable Shielded VM secure boot in instance templates
requiredActions: Set shielded_instance_config.enable_secure_boot = true in the instance template.

rules.threat: An instance template with an access_config block assigns public IPs to every VM in the group, exposing the whole fleet directly to the internet.
rules.title: Do not assign public IPs in instance templates

remediate.snippet: Remove the access_config block so VMs created from the template have no external IP.

requiredActions: Remove the access_config block from the instance template network_interface.

rules.threat: delete_contents_on_destroy lets a single Terraform destroy wipe every table in the dataset, turning an accidental or malicious apply into total data loss.
rules.title: Do not enable delete_contents_on_destroy on BigQuery datasets
requiredActions: Set delete_contents_on_destroy = false on the BigQuery dataset.

rules.threat: Vertex AI workbench instances reachable over a public IP expose notebook environments and attached credentials to internet-borne attacks.
rules.title: Disable public IP on Vertex AI Workbench instances

remediate.snippet: Set gce_setup.disable_public_ip = true on the Workbench instance.

requiredActions: Set disable_public_ip = true on the Vertex AI Workbench instance.

rules.threat: A Cloud Armor security policy whose default rule allows all traffic provides no baseline protection; only explicitly blocked patterns are stopped, leaving everything else through.
rules.title: Avoid default-allow on Cloud Armor security policies

remediate.snippet: Define explicit rules (including a default rule with appropriate action) on the security policy.

requiredActions: Define explicit rules with a restrictive default action on the Cloud Armor security policy.

rules.threat: Setting log_min_duration_statement to 0 logs every query including those containing sensitive parameters, which can leak secrets into logs an attacker may read.
rules.title: Do not log all statements via log_min_duration_statement=0 on Cloud SQL

remediate.snippet: Set log_min_duration_statement to -1 (disabled) or a positive threshold rather than 0.

requiredActions: Set the log_min_duration_statement flag to -1 or a positive value, not 0.

rules.threat: Dataproc clusters without Kerberos lack strong authentication between Hadoop components, allowing an attacker on the network to impersonate services and access data.
rules.title: Enable Kerberos on Dataproc clusters

remediate.snippet: Add a security_config.kerberos_config block to enable Kerberos authentication.

requiredActions: Add a kerberos_config block under security_config on the Dataproc cluster.

rules.threat: A node pool exposing the kubelet read-only port (10255) lets any pod or local attacker read node and pod metadata without authentication, aiding reconnaissance and escalation.
rules.title: Disable the kubelet read-only port on GKE node pools
requiredActions: Set kubelet_config.insecure_kubelet_readonly_port_enabled = false on the node pool.

rules.threat: A Cloud Run service that omits a dedicated service account runs as the default Compute SA with broad project permissions, so a code-exec bug becomes project-wide compromise.
rules.title: Assign a dedicated service account to Cloud Run services

remediate.snippet: Set template.service_account to a dedicated least-privilege service account.

requiredActions: Set a dedicated service_account on the Cloud Run v2 service template.

rules.threat: A firewall rule allowing all protocols (all) from 0.0.0.0/0 effectively disables the perimeter, exposing every port on targeted instances to the internet.
rules.title: Do not allow all protocols from the internet in firewall rules

remediate.snippet: Replace the protocol = "all" allow rule with specific protocols/ports and restricted source_ranges.

requiredActions: Restrict the firewall rule to specific protocols/ports and trusted source_ranges.

rules.threat: Granting roles to the default Compute/App Engine service account concentrates excessive privilege on an account attached to many resources, amplifying blast radius of any single compromise.
rules.title: Do not grant elevated roles to default service accounts

remediate.snippet: Grant roles to dedicated least-privilege service accounts instead of the default SAs.

requiredActions: Remove role grants to default service accounts in the project IAM member binding.

rules.threat: An attacker negotiates a weak or legacy TLS cipher against an SSL proxy load balancer that has no SSL policy attached, downgrading the connection to intercept traffic.
rules.title: Attach a restrictive SSL policy to SSL proxy load balancers
requiredActions: Create a google_compute_ssl_policy with min_tls_version TLS_1_2 and profile MODERN or RESTRICTED and reference it via ssl_policy

rules.threat: An attacker who exfiltrates a disk snapshot stored with Google-managed keys can restore it in an attacker-controlled project, since no customer key is required to decrypt it.
rules.title: Encrypt compute snapshots with customer-managed keys

remediate.snippet: Add a snapshot_encryption_key block referencing a Cloud KMS key.

requiredActions: Add a snapshot_encryption_key block with kms_key_self_link pointing to a customer-managed Cloud KMS key

rules.threat: An attacker who gains read access to a custom image encrypted only with Google-managed keys can clone it into another project, exposing baked-in secrets and configuration.
rules.title: Encrypt compute images with customer-managed keys

remediate.snippet: Add an image_encryption_key block referencing a Cloud KMS key.

requiredActions: Add an image_encryption_key block with kms_key_self_link pointing to a customer-managed Cloud KMS key

rules.threat: An auto-mode VPC silently creates a subnet in every region, expanding the network attack surface into regions the team never intended to use and complicating firewall governance.
rules.title: Disable auto-created subnetworks on VPC networks
requiredActions: Set auto_create_subnetworks = false and define subnetworks explicitly per region in use

rules.threat: Without project-wide OS Login, instances fall back to metadata SSH keys that are not tied to IAM, letting a compromised key grant durable, unaudited shell access across the project.
rules.title: Enable OS Login at the project metadata level

remediate.snippet: Add enable-oslogin = "TRUE" to the project metadata map.

requiredActions: Add enable-oslogin = "TRUE" to the metadata map of google_compute_project_metadata

rules.threat: Project-level SSH keys in metadata grant broad shell access to every instance; an attacker who adds a key or steals an existing one moves laterally across all VMs.
rules.title: Block project-wide SSH keys via project metadata

remediate.snippet: Add block-project-ssh-keys = "TRUE" to the project metadata map.

requiredActions: Add block-project-ssh-keys = "TRUE" to the metadata map of google_compute_project_metadata

rules.threat: Without Cloud Armor Adaptive Protection, a volumetric Layer 7 DDoS attack against the application can saturate the backend before static rules ever trigger.
rules.title: Enable Cloud Armor Adaptive Protection for L7 DDoS

remediate.snippet: Add adaptive_protection_config with layer_7_ddos_defense_config enable = true.

requiredActions: Add an adaptive_protection_config block enabling layer_7_ddos_defense_config

rules.threat: Without authenticator group configuration, RBAC cannot bind Kubernetes roles to Google Groups, forcing per-user grants that drift and leave stale cluster-admin access.
rules.title: Configure Google Groups for GKE RBAC

remediate.snippet: Add authenticator_groups_config with the gke-security-groups group.

requiredActions: Add an authenticator_groups_config block with a security_group binding to gke-security-groups@yourdomain

rules.threat: Without managed mesh certificates, service-to-service mTLS in the cluster relies on manually rotated certs that expire or leak, enabling impersonation between workloads.
rules.title: Enable managed mesh certificates on GKE

remediate.snippet: Add mesh_certificates with enable_certificates = true.

requiredActions: Add a mesh_certificates block with enable_certificates = true and Workload Identity enabled

rules.threat: Without workload vulnerability scanning, an attacker can deploy containers with known-exploitable packages that go undetected until they are used for initial access.
rules.title: Enable GKE workload vulnerability scanning in security posture

remediate.snippet: Set vulnerability_mode in security_posture_config.

requiredActions: Set vulnerability_mode to VULNERABILITY_BASIC or VULNERABILITY_ENTERPRISE inside security_posture_config

rules.threat: Local SSD scratch data written in cleartext can be recovered from the underlying host; without confidential storage encryption, an attacker with host access reads ephemeral workload data.
rules.title: Enable confidential storage on GKE node pools

remediate.snippet: Set enable_confidential_storage = true inside node_config (requires local SSD).

requiredActions: Set enable_confidential_storage = true in node_config and attach local NVMe SSDs

rules.threat: Node VMs that accept project-wide SSH keys give an attacker a direct shell onto Kubernetes hosts, bypassing the control plane and exposing kubelet credentials.
rules.title: Block project SSH keys on GKE node pools

remediate.snippet: Add block-project-ssh-keys = "true" to node_config.metadata.

requiredActions: Add block-project-ssh-keys = "true" to the metadata map in node_config

rules.threat: Without statement logging, an attacker performing schema changes or privilege escalation via DDL leaves no audit trail, defeating post-incident forensics.
rules.title: Log DDL statements on Cloud SQL PostgreSQL

remediate.snippet: Add a database_flags block log_statement = ddl (or mod).

requiredActions: Add a database_flags entry with name = "log_statement" and value = "ddl" or stricter

rules.threat: Terse error verbosity hides query context an investigator needs to reconstruct an attacker's failed injection attempts and probing.
rules.title: Set adequate error verbosity on Cloud SQL PostgreSQL

remediate.snippet: Add database_flags log_error_verbosity = default.

requiredActions: Add a database_flags entry with name = "log_error_verbosity" and value = "default" or "verbose"

rules.threat: Without hostname logging, connection log entries show only IPs, hampering correlation of an attacker's source host across audit records.
rules.title: Log client hostnames on Cloud SQL PostgreSQL

remediate.snippet: Add database_flags log_hostname = on.

requiredActions: Add a database_flags entry with name = "log_hostname" and value = "on"

rules.threat: Unlogged temp file creation hides resource-exhaustion or data-exfiltration queries that spill large result sets to disk.
rules.title: Log temporary files on Cloud SQL PostgreSQL

remediate.snippet: Add database_flags log_temp_files = 0 to log all temp files.

requiredActions: Add a database_flags entry with name = "log_temp_files" and value = "0"

rules.threat: Without a built-in password policy, weak or reused database passwords survive, letting an attacker brute-force or credential-stuff the instance.
rules.title: Enforce a password policy on Cloud SQL instances

remediate.snippet: Add a password_validation_policy block with enable_password_policy = true.

requiredActions: Add a settings.password_validation_policy block with enable_password_policy = true and min_length >= 14

rules.threat: An instance that still accepts unencrypted connections lets a network attacker sniff or MITM database traffic; require_ssl alone does not enforce the stronger ssl_mode.
rules.title: Require encrypted-only SSL mode on Cloud SQL

remediate.snippet: Set ssl_mode = ENCRYPTED_ONLY in settings.ip_configuration.

requiredActions: Set ssl_mode to ENCRYPTED_ONLY or TRUSTED_CLIENT_CERTIFICATE_REQUIRED in ip_configuration

rules.threat: Without a private path for Google Cloud services, internal GCP service traffic to the database can traverse public IP routing, broadening exposure.
rules.title: Enable private path for Google Cloud services on Cloud SQL

remediate.snippet: Set enable_private_path_for_google_cloud_services = true in ip_configuration.

requiredActions: Set enable_private_path_for_google_cloud_services = true in settings.ip_configuration

rules.threat: Without a default event-based hold, objects can be deleted before a retention event is set, letting an attacker destroy evidence immediately after writing it.
rules.title: Set default event-based hold on sensitive buckets
requiredActions: Set default_event_based_hold = true on the google_storage_bucket

rules.threat: An open Dataproc component-gateway HTTP port exposes cluster web UIs (YARN, Spark) to the internet, leaking job data and offering a foothold for code execution.
rules.title: Disable Dataproc component HTTP port access

remediate.snippet: Set enable_http_port_access = false in cluster_config.endpoint_config.

requiredActions: Set enable_http_port_access = false in endpoint_config or remove the component gateway

rules.threat: Dataflow pipeline state and shuffle data encrypted only with Google-managed keys can be read by anyone who compromises the service, with no customer key boundary.
rules.title: Encrypt Dataflow jobs with a customer-managed key
requiredActions: Set kms_key_name to a customer-managed Cloud KMS crypto key id

rules.threat: Without Binary Authorization, an attacker who can push to the registry deploys an unsigned, tampered image to Cloud Run with no admission control.
rules.title: Enforce Binary Authorization on Cloud Run v2 services

remediate.snippet: Add a binary_authorization block referencing a policy.

requiredActions: Add a binary_authorization block (use_default = true or a policy) and define a deploy policy that requires attestations

rules.threat: A Cloud Run revision encrypted only with Google-managed keys offers no customer-controlled key revocation if the workload or its data is compromised.
rules.title: Encrypt Cloud Run v2 services with a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Set template.encryption_key to a Cloud KMS key.

requiredActions: Set encryption_key in the service template to a customer-managed Cloud KMS key id

rules.threat: A function with ALL_TRAFFIC egress not pinned to the VPC connector can reach the internet directly, enabling data exfiltration that bypasses network controls.
rules.title: Route all Cloud Function egress through the VPC connector
requiredActions: Set vpc_connector_egress_settings = "ALL_TRAFFIC" so all egress traverses the VPC connector

rules.threat: A 2nd-gen function with no VPC connector reaches resources over public networking, expanding the egress attack surface and bypassing private-network controls.
rules.title: Attach a VPC connector to 2nd-gen Cloud Functions

remediate.snippet: Set service_config.vpc_connector to a Serverless VPC Access connector.

requiredActions: Set vpc_connector in service_config to a google_vpc_access_connector id

rules.threat: A Vertex AI index endpoint without CMEK stores embedding data under Google-managed keys, removing the customer's ability to revoke access to sensitive vector data.
rules.title: Encrypt Vertex AI index endpoints with a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Add an encryption_spec block with kms_key_name.

requiredActions: Add an encryption_spec block with kms_key_name set to a customer-managed Cloud KMS key

rules.threat: ML metadata (lineage, parameters) in a Vertex AI metadata store without CMEK cannot be cryptographically isolated, exposing training provenance if Google-managed keys are compromised.
rules.title: Encrypt Vertex AI metadata stores with a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Add an encryption_spec block with kms_key_name.

requiredActions: Add an encryption_spec block with kms_key_name set to a customer-managed Cloud KMS key

rules.threat: Tensorboard experiment data without CMEK is encrypted only with Google keys, denying the customer the ability to revoke access to potentially sensitive training artifacts.
rules.title: Encrypt Vertex AI Tensorboards with a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Add an encryption_spec block with kms_key_name.

requiredActions: Add an encryption_spec block with kms_key_name set to a customer-managed Cloud KMS key

rules.threat: A Vertex AI Workbench notebook boot/data disk under Google-managed keys exposes notebook contents and credentials if the platform key boundary is breached.
rules.title: Encrypt notebook instance disks with a customer-managed key
requiredActions: Set disk_encryption = "CMEK" and kms_key to a customer-managed Cloud KMS key id

rules.threat: Leaving the notebook proxy access enabled exposes a public Jupyter URL; an attacker who guesses or steals the proxy URL gains an interactive code-execution session.
rules.title: Disable proxy access on notebook instances
requiredActions: Set no_proxy_access = true on the google_notebooks_instance

rules.threat: Long-lived service account keys are the top GCP credential-theft vector; without the constraint, any project member can mint a downloadable key that never expires.
rules.title: Enforce org policy disabling service account key creation

remediate.snippet: Set the boolean rule enforce = TRUE for iam.disableServiceAccountKeyCreation.

requiredActions: Set spec.rules.enforce = TRUE for the iam.disableServiceAccountKeyCreation constraint

rules.threat: Without the vmExternalIpAccess constraint enforced, any VM can be given a public IP, exposing it directly to internet scanning and attack.
rules.title: Enforce org policy restricting VM external IP access

remediate.snippet: Set a deny_all = true list rule for compute.vmExternalIpAccess.

requiredActions: Set spec.rules with deny_all = true for the compute.vmExternalIpAccess list constraint

rules.threat: Without enforcing compute.requireOsLogin org-wide, projects can fall back to metadata SSH keys that bypass IAM-based access auditing.
rules.title: Enforce org policy requiring OS Login

remediate.snippet: Set the boolean rule enforce = TRUE for compute.requireOsLogin.

requiredActions: Set spec.rules.enforce = TRUE for the compute.requireOsLogin constraint

rules.threat: Without enforcing compute.requireShieldedVm, attackers can launch VMs lacking secure boot and vTPM, enabling rootkit persistence undetected by integrity monitoring.
rules.title: Enforce org policy requiring Shielded VM

remediate.snippet: Set the boolean rule enforce = TRUE for compute.requireShieldedVm.

requiredActions: Set spec.rules.enforce = TRUE for the compute.requireShieldedVm constraint

rules.threat: Without enforcing storage.uniformBucketLevelAccess, buckets can use legacy ACLs that grant object-level public access invisible to IAM-based policy review.
rules.title: Enforce org policy requiring uniform bucket-level access

remediate.snippet: Set the boolean rule enforce = TRUE for storage.uniformBucketLevelAccess.

requiredActions: Set spec.rules.enforce = TRUE for the storage.uniformBucketLevelAccess constraint

rules.threat: Serial-port access offers an out-of-band console that bypasses SSH controls; without compute.disableSerialPortAccess enforced, an attacker with metadata access can open an interactive serial session.
rules.title: Enforce org policy disabling serial port access

remediate.snippet: Set the boolean rule enforce = TRUE for compute.disableSerialPortAccess.

requiredActions: Set spec.rules.enforce = TRUE for the compute.disableSerialPortAccess constraint

rules.threat: Unrestricted VPC peering lets an attacker who controls another project bridge networks into yours, exposing internal services across the peering boundary.
rules.title: Enforce org policy restricting VPC peering

remediate.snippet: Replace allow_all with an allowed_values list of approved networks for compute.restrictVpcPeering.

requiredActions: Replace allow_all = true with an explicit allowed_values list for the compute.restrictVpcPeering constraint

rules.threat: A Binary Authorization policy whose default rule allows all images defeats the control entirely, letting an attacker deploy unsigned malicious containers cluster-wide.
rules.title: Require attestation in Binary Authorization default rule

remediate.snippet: Set default_admission_rule evaluation_mode = REQUIRE_ATTESTATION and enforcement_mode = ENFORCED_BLOCK_AND_AUDIT_LOG.

requiredActions: Set the default_admission_rule evaluation_mode to REQUIRE_ATTESTATION with ENFORCED_BLOCK_AND_AUDIT_LOG enforcement

rules.threat: A non-private Data Fusion instance exposes its pipeline UI and tenant project over public networking, broadening the attack surface for the data-integration plane.
rules.title: Make Data Fusion instances private
requiredActions: Set private_instance = true and supply a network_config for private peering

rules.threat: Without Stackdriver logging enabled, Data Fusion pipeline executions leave no centralized audit trail, blinding detection of malicious or anomalous data movement.
rules.title: Enable Stackdriver logging on Data Fusion instances
requiredActions: Set enable_stackdriver_logging = true on the google_data_fusion_instance

rules.threat: Without monitoring, resource-abuse or crypto-mining workloads injected into Data Fusion go unnoticed until billing or downstream impact surfaces.
rules.title: Enable Stackdriver monitoring on Data Fusion instances
requiredActions: Set enable_stackdriver_monitoring = true on the google_data_fusion_instance

rules.threat: A Cloud Composer (Airflow) environment with a public GKE control plane and web server exposes DAG orchestration to the internet, a high-value target for code execution.
rules.title: Make Cloud Composer environments private

remediate.snippet: Add private_environment_config with enable_private_endpoint = true.

requiredActions: Add a config.private_environment_config block with enable_private_endpoint = true

rules.threat: A Composer environment without CMEK encrypts Airflow metadata and DAG state under Google keys, denying customer key revocation if the orchestration plane is compromised.
rules.title: Encrypt Cloud Composer environments with a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Add config.encryption_config with kms_key_name.

requiredActions: Add a config.encryption_config block with kms_key_name set to a customer-managed Cloud KMS key

rules.threat: Short or default message retention means an attacker who triggers redelivery or replay leaves no window for forensic recovery of the original messages.
rules.title: Set explicit message retention on Pub/Sub subscriptions
requiredActions: Set message_retention_duration explicitly on the subscription

rules.threat: An Artifact Registry repo granted to allUsers lets anyone pull (or with write roles, poison) container images, enabling supply-chain compromise of every consumer.
rules.title: Prevent public IAM bindings on Artifact Registry repositories

remediate.snippet: Remove allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers and bind to specific principals.

requiredActions: Remove allUsers and allAuthenticatedUsers members; grant repository access only to named principals

rules.threat: Granting actAs/serviceAccountUser to allUsers lets anyone impersonate the service account, inheriting all of its IAM privileges for privilege escalation.
rules.title: Prevent public impersonation of service accounts

remediate.snippet: Remove allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers from the service account IAM member.

requiredActions: Remove allUsers and allAuthenticatedUsers from google_service_account_iam_member bindings

rules.threat: An audit config scoped to a single service leaves other APIs unlogged; an attacker pivots to an unaudited service to operate without leaving Data Access logs.
rules.title: Apply audit logging to all services
requiredActions: Set service = "allServices" with DATA_READ and DATA_WRITE audit_log_config entries

rules.threat: A default object ACL granting allUsers makes every newly uploaded object world-readable, silently leaking data the moment it is written.
rules.title: Prevent public default object ACLs on buckets

remediate.snippet: Remove allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers from the default object ACL; prefer uniform bucket-level access.

requiredActions: Remove allUsers and allAuthenticatedUsers entities from default object ACLs and enable uniform bucket-level access

rules.threat: Without log_duration, slow or abusive queries leave no timing trace, hindering detection of injection-driven enumeration or denial-of-service patterns.
rules.title: Enable statement duration logging on Cloud SQL PostgreSQL

remediate.snippet: Add database_flags log_duration = on.

requiredActions: Add a database_flags entry with name = "log_duration" and value = "on"

rules.threat: An SSL proxy whose attached policy permits TLS 1.0 lets an attacker force a downgrade to a deprecated protocol vulnerable to known cipher attacks.
rules.title: Reject TLS 1.0 on SSL policies used by proxies
requiredActions: Set min_tls_version to TLS_1_2 on the SSL policy

rules.threat: The auto-created default internet route on a VPC permits direct egress to the internet; leaving it enables data exfiltration from instances that should be isolated.
rules.title: Delete default internet routes on isolated VPCs
requiredActions: Set delete_default_routes_on_create = true and add only the egress routes you require

rules.threat: Cluster node-pool defaults without Shielded VM let auto-provisioned node pools boot unverified images, enabling firmware/bootkit persistence on nodes.
rules.title: Set Shielded defaults for auto-provisioned GKE node pools

remediate.snippet: Add node_pool_defaults to set secure defaults for node auto-provisioning.

requiredActions: Add a node_pool_defaults block establishing Shielded and secure node_config defaults

rules.threat: A Dataflow Flex Template job with public-IP workers exposes worker VMs to the internet, expanding the attack surface of the data-processing fleet.
rules.title: Disable public IPs on Dataflow Flex Template workers
requiredActions: Set ip_configuration = "WORKER_IP_PRIVATE" on the Flex Template job

rules.threat: Flex Template job state under Google-managed keys cannot be cryptographically isolated, removing the customer's ability to revoke access to in-flight data.
rules.title: Encrypt Dataflow Flex Template jobs with a customer-managed key
requiredActions: Set kms_key_name to a customer-managed Cloud KMS crypto key id

rules.threat: A Vertex AI endpoint serving a model without CMEK keeps deployed model artifacts under Google keys, removing customer revocation if the serving plane is compromised.
rules.title: Encrypt Vertex AI endpoints with a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Add an encryption_spec block with kms_key_name.

requiredActions: Add an encryption_spec block with kms_key_name set to a customer-managed Cloud KMS key

rules.threat: A Redis instance without transit encryption in a non-private connect mode exposes cache contents, including session tokens, to network sniffing.
rules.title: Require server-authentication transit encryption on Memorystore Redis
requiredActions: Set transit_encryption_mode to SERVER_AUTHENTICATION (not DISABLED)

rules.threat: An overly broad deny-maintenance period blocks security patches indefinitely, leaving the database exposed to known CVEs an attacker can exploit.
rules.title: Avoid open-ended Cloud SQL maintenance deny periods

remediate.snippet: Define a maintenance_window so security patches apply on a schedule.

requiredActions: Define a settings.maintenance_window with an update_track so patches apply regularly

rules.threat: A BigQuery table-level IAM grant to allUsers exposes potentially sensitive rows to the entire internet for unauthenticated querying.
rules.title: Prevent public IAM bindings on BigQuery tables

remediate.snippet: Remove allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers from the table IAM member.

requiredActions: Remove allUsers and allAuthenticatedUsers from google_bigquery_table_iam_member bindings

rules.threat: A Secret Manager secret granted to allUsers exposes credentials, API keys, or tokens to anyone, an immediate full-credential compromise.
rules.title: Prevent public IAM bindings on Secret Manager secrets

remediate.snippet: Remove allUsers/allAuthenticatedUsers from the secret IAM member.

requiredActions: Remove allUsers and allAuthenticatedUsers from google_secret_manager_secret_iam_member bindings

rules.threat: A region disk created without a customer-managed key cannot have its data access revoked independently of Google, leaving exfiltrated volumes decryptable.
rules.title: Encrypt regional persistent disks with a customer-managed key

remediate.snippet: Add a disk_encryption_key block with kms_key_name.

requiredActions: Add a disk_encryption_key block with kms_key_name set to a customer-managed Cloud KMS key

rules.threat: A custom route to the default internet gateway with a 0.0.0.0/0 destination opens unrestricted egress, enabling data exfiltration from supposedly isolated subnets.
rules.title: Avoid broad internet-gateway routes on isolated networks

remediate.snippet: Scope dest_range to required CIDRs or route egress via a NAT/proxy instead of the default internet gateway.

requiredActions: Replace the 0.0.0.0/0 default-internet-gateway route with narrowly scoped egress routes

rules.threat: GKE node pools left with the legacy kubelet config defaults can expose an unauthenticated read-only port; absent an explicit kubelet_config the cluster relies on insecure defaults.
rules.title: Set an explicit kubelet configuration on GKE node pools

remediate.snippet: Add a kubelet_config block and disable the read-only port.

requiredActions: Add a node_config.kubelet_config block and set insecure_kubelet_readonly_port_enabled = false

rules.threat: A Cloud Run v2 service that allows all ingress is reachable directly from the internet, bypassing any load balancer WAF and exposing the app to direct attack.
rules.title: Restrict Cloud Run v2 ingress away from all traffic
requiredActions: Set ingress to INGRESS_TRAFFIC_INTERNAL_ONLY or INGRESS_TRAFFIC_INTERNAL_LOAD_BALANCER

rules.threat: Without a dead-letter topic, poison messages that crash consumers redeliver indefinitely, an availability attack vector and a blind spot for malformed-payload detection.
rules.title: Configure a dead-letter policy on Pub/Sub subscriptions

remediate.snippet: Add a dead_letter_policy block referencing a DLQ topic.

requiredActions: Add a dead_letter_policy block with a dead_letter_topic and max_delivery_attempts

rules.threat: A forwarding rule with EXTERNAL load balancing scheme publishes the backend directly to the internet; using INTERNAL keeps the service on private networking.
rules.title: Prefer internal load balancing scheme on forwarding rules

remediate.snippet: Use load_balancing_scheme = INTERNAL or place the EXTERNAL rule behind Cloud Armor.

requiredActions: Set load_balancing_scheme to INTERNAL or INTERNAL_MANAGED, or attach Cloud Armor if external exposure is required

rules.threat: A Bigtable table without deletion protection can be wiped by a compromised credential, destroying operational data with no built-in safeguard.
rules.title: Enable deletion protection on Bigtable tables
requiredActions: Set deletion_protection = "PROTECTED" on the google_bigtable_table
